title: Arthropteris orientalis
intro: Arthropteris orientalis, also known as the Oriental maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arthropteris orientalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Arthropteris orientalis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular disorders.
taxonomy: Arthropteris orientalis belongs to the family Marattiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the leather ferns. The family Marattiaceae is part of the class Polypodiopsida, also known as the polypod ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is further categorized under the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns. The division Pteridophyta is a subset of the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants. Arthropteris orientalis is a rare and endemic species of fern found in Asia.
